reviews.review_description:
This game is really fun and honestly one of my favorites to play in VR. The atmosphere, gameplay, and overall experience are super exciting, especially if you enjoy Backrooms-style games. It can be intense and really entertaining, and I think a lot of people would love it.

However, one thing that could make the game much better is the multiplayer system. Right now, you can only play alone or join friends by entering a code. It would be awesome if there was an open online mode where you could automatically join games with other players. That would make the experience more social, more unpredictable, and a lot more fun.

I really believe this game has huge potential, and adding better online matchmaking or public lobbies would bring in even more players and make it even more enjoyable.

Overall, amazing game — just needs improved online features!

The levels are somewhat scary very fun played with a friend, although there are no public servers for me. I don’t know why I do wish that the developers add more levels and make a better ending cause me and my friend spent 15 minutes making sure that the end level was actually the end and please also make the pool room easier 
￼￼￼

Other than that it is a fun game to play with your friends

People are complaining about some stuff but i know that the game isnt even finished yet! That makes the game like 20 times better because the game is already excellent, but the fact that its still in early access means that better stuff like multiplayer and more levels is still to come!

Listen, I think this is a great game with a good concept and I really, really like the atmosphere and physiological aspect of the game, but the main issue is that the map is way to big. Me and my friend spent 45 min trying to find 5 vhs tapes around the map and only got 2 of them. Also, I love the monster, but you barely see it because of how big the map is. If there was one thing I would suggest to the devs if they see it is please make the map smaller, so people can see what the rest of your game actually has to offer.

The game is very well made and the graphics are actually phenomenal, it feels like I’m in the actually backrooms video. 

Only thing is it’s decently laggy throughout the game, but that’s understandable considering how big the map is.

Wonder VR is an amazing liminal horror exploration game because it creates a world that feels strange, mysterious, and unforgettable from the very first moment. It does not need to rely only on loud scares or constant action to be exciting. Instead, it builds fear through atmosphere, emptiness, and curiosity. The game makes ordinary spaces feel unusual, as if every room, hallway, wall, and dark corner has a secret hidden inside it. That is what makes Wonder VR so special. It takes the familiar and turns it into something unsettling, creating a powerful feeling that stays with the player even after they stop playing. The environments look simple at first, but that simplicity actually makes the game more effective. The quiet rooms, dim lighting, and strange designs make players feel like they have entered a dreamlike place where something is slightly wrong. This gives Wonder VR a unique identity and makes it stand out from many other horror games.

One of the best things about Wonder VR is how immersive it feels. Because it is a VR experience, players are not just watching the game happen on a screen. They are placed directly inside the world. That makes every sound, movement, and shadow feel much more intense. Walking through a creepy hallway in a normal game can be scary, but walking through that hallway in virtual reality feels completely different. It feels personal, immediate, and real. Wonder VR uses this feeling extremely well. It makes the player feel surrounded by the environment, which adds to the suspense and horror. The player has to look around, move forward, and explore the space themselves. This makes the fear stronger because the player is responsible for their own movement and choices.

Wonder VR is also amazing because it captures the feeling of liminal spaces so well. Liminal spaces are places that feel empty, transitional, or strangely familiar, like abandoned hallways, empty rooms, quiet buildings, or areas that seem like they should have people.

Ok, I don’t write reviews often, but I just got off this game and there are some things that I have to give this game credit for.
I played this game originally a while ago and I was honestly disappointed because of the dropoff of quality from the first level to the second one. This recent update that added multiplayer, I got back on because I started watching the original backrooms series. I joined in a lobby and played through it again. This time I got farther than I ever had, and this game became so much better.
The game genuinely feels like you and the bros are getting lost in the threshold. The levels are pretty well done after the second one. The poolrooms are some of the best I’ve see in any game and are really well done as well. The immersion is great and are well balanced. Multiplayer lobbies made this game infinitely better. Please keep developing this game, and download this rn

I have played many backrooms games in my time on VR, some were good, many were bad, but then i came across WONDER. VR ©. I don’t even know where to start, the amazing graphics, well crafted levels, the amazing Staff Team, this game has it all. Now there is many bugs and some of them completely ruin the your run, but putting aside the bad and only looking at the good, its perfect. 9.3/10

-Mr. Ketchup

NOT A BOT

This is the best backrooms game I've seen on vr so far, and its only in early access! I would recommend to give it look, and im very excited for it's upcoming updates.

In game thingy things:
The game has an active community suggesting ideas and they hear them! The game has amazing graphics for both quest 2 and quest 3s/3. The gameplay is enjoyable and a bit long. It's great for brining along a couple friends and exploring the backrooms! I personally have not experienced any huge bugs while playing and I've been playing for a long while now. The attention to details are great, an example is how the visual of the hand move against surfaces.

reviews.review_title: Easily one of the GOATs of VR.
reviews.review_description:
This game does something most backrooms games fail to do: feel like a proper backrooms game. Even just the small things like the flash light, and the vhs visuals, and the sound effects and ambiance all come together to make the game immerse you. This game keeps getting updated, and gets better with each one. Games like this are where the VR industry needs to be, simple, fun, and true to the source material with devs that listen to the community. 

Fantastic job Chronicle Studios. 5 stars. 


(And if anyone cares, I go more in depth into where vr should be headed for the future, and how games like this improve, and strengthen the gorilla-VR games genre in my yt video on the topic. https://youtu.be/YRFo-IOz7do?si=7Rh6ANHlujaDdQhM )

Hey Timmy!

We apologize for the photos convincing you that it was stick movement. A point that we would like to get out is with enough courage and playtime you can learn how to run. We're still taking your feedback and will work towards a solution to make it not look like a stick movement game. 

Thank you for the review/feedback!

developer_response.body:
Hello Player!

We're sorry to hear about your experience when playing our game and really will use your feedback to our advantage to ensure a better experience next time you play. As of now we have added public lobby functionality while also adding more levels.

Please let us know if this update has upped your review at all, and if it has not then please let us know any other complaints you have.

reviews.review_description: This game looks great to begin, but the gameplay is simply not made for users. Certain parts are fun, but the AI is not great and some levels are boring, especially pool rooms. In poolrooms, you have to do extremely hard parkour that the average player CANNOT do. All of my friends couldn't, and I also couldn't. I understand it's beta, but that's a silly mistake that seems like a joke. Before releasing your SECOND beta, mind you, please put more thought into user experience.
